sections.content:
Good, the board is back to the initial state (all slots empty). Now let me try the palette order hypothesis.
Palette: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y (left to right)
Red slots (left to right): R, p, N
Green slots (left to right): O, M, b, Y
Let me fill and then press SPACE to test.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Red slots: R, p, N; Green slots: O, M, b, Y

    # Red: R, p, N

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 17},  # p

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 24},  # N

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 52},  # Y
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 40},  # green slot 4
    'SPACE'
]

action(fill_palette, intent='solve')
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
